text.title: growth-referral-programs Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for growth-referral-programs. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Default to double-sided rewards (both referrer and referee benefit); single-sided programs feel exploitative and convert 20-30% worse on the invitee side.
rule.rationale: The invitee receiving a reward removes the psychological burden of accepting a favor and signals the program is legitimate, not just a multi-level scheme.

rule.statement: Size the reward at 20-50% of CAC; recompute as CAC drifts rather than fixing a round-number amount permanently.
rule.rationale: A reward below 20% of CAC is often not motivating. Above 50%, the economics deteriorate relative to paid channels. LTV must exceed 3x CAC for a double-sided program to be sustainable.

rule.statement: Ask for referrals at win moments: post-purchase, after a positive review, after a support win, at feature milestones, at subscription renewal.
rule.rationale: Satisfaction is at its peak at win moments. Users are primed to share when they have just experienced value — not on first visit, not during checkout.

rule.statement: Provide a one-click copy link as the primary sharing mechanism; add email, social, and in-app invite as secondary options. Long referral links must be shortened.
rule.rationale: Every extra click in the sharing flow reduces i by 10-20%. Copy-link is the highest-participation channel because it requires no authentication or form.

rule.statement: Use deterministic fraud rules (IP overlap, payment-method match, signup velocity) rather than LLM judgment; flag candidates for human review rather than auto-banning.
rule.rationale: LLMs are not reliable fraud adjudicators. Deterministic feature computation is auditable and consistent; final ban decisions require a human-in-loop to avoid legal exposure.
